{{Quote|During this time of celebration for our magnificent Moff, the city is alive with entertainment. We are here to drink to his health and wish him well… even if he can't be here to join us.|Mosh Pelkan|<ref name="CC5" />}}
The '''Mephitisians''' were a four-[[Limb/Legends|armed]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mephitis]]. Their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] had a noxious [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], and as a consequence, the species had evolved adaptations to limit exposure to these gases, such as lids over their [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and the lack of a [[Nose/Legends|nose]].

The Mephitisian [[Mosh Pelkan]] gained prominence on the planet [[Questal/Legends|Questal]] as a [[Turf Boss]]—a criminal leader with quasi-official authority. He hosted a celebration for the [[Birthday/Legends|birthday]] of local [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Bandor (Moff)|Bandor]], which was infiltrated by [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] agents.

==Biology and appearance==
The Mephitisians were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] with [[Color/Legends|purple]]-tinged skin and four [[Limb/Legends|arms]], each of which they could use independently of the others. Having evolved on a [[Planet/Legends|world]] with a noxious smell, they had adapted to their environment: the Mephitisians had no noses, so they lacked the ability to smell, and their black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] were small and covered with clear lids. They had large upper lips that they flapped over their [[Mouth/Legends|mouths]] to limit their exposure to the air. Their heads had two horn-like projections, which were sensitive to vibrations and air movements. The Mephitisians were dexterous beings.<ref name="CC5" />

==History==
[[File:Cromag.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Mosh Pelkan's champion, Cromag]]
The Mephitisians evolved on the planet [[Mephitis]],<ref name="CC5" /> located in the southwestern portion of the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]].<ref name="TEAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> The [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] on their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was noxious and offensive to all species that visited the planet. The Mephitisians were part of the [[galactic community]] from the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

The Mephitisian [[Mosh Pelkan]] was a [[Turf Boss]] on the planet [[Questal/Legends|Questal]]. Turf Bosses were criminal leaders who had agreements with the official [[District Commander]]s to keep down insurgency in exchange for the Commanders turning a blind eye to their activities. Originally traveling to the planet with the intention of becoming an [[ardanium]] prospector, Pelkan soon realized that [[Smuggler/Legends|importing]] illegal goods earned a greater profit. He established a significant power base and was a popular figure in [[Gralleenya]], Questal's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]]. When [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Bandor (Moff)|Bandor]] was given jurisdiction over Questal, he gave Pelkan control over a [[district]] of Gralleenya.<ref name="CC5" />

Pelkan hosted a costume gala at [[Pelkan's Mansion|his mansion]] to celebrate Bandor's [[Birthday/Legends|birthday]]. The party was infiltrated by agents of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], who were searching for the missing agent [[Tay Vanis|Tiree]]. The Rebels believed Pelkan could provide them information on Tiree on the advice of local [[Cantina/Legends|bar]] owner [[Tan Shilley]], who had earlier suggested to Tiree that he contact Pelkan. At Pelkan's party, entertainment was offered in the form of [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiatorial]] combat.<ref name="CC5" />

{{RPG}}
Despite their best efforts, the Rebel agents gained no useful information from Pelkan, but were instead forced to fight in the arena against his champion, [[Cromag]]. They defeated Cromag and escaped the party, eventually rescuing Tiree from where he was being held in [[Bandor's Palace]].<ref name="CC5" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
The Mephitisians were created by [[Robert Kern]] for the [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure ''[[The Game Chambers of Questal]]'', published by [[West End Games]] as a standalone paperback in [[1990]], and as part of the collection ''[[Classic Adventures: Volume Five]]'' in [[1995]]. In the scene involving the Mephitisian Mosh Pelkan, there are five examples of why the players might end up fighting Cromag; whether one of these or an original reason, the fight is a set part of the adventure.

In [[Wikipedia:Roman mythology|Roman mythology]], Mefitas was a goddess synonymous with [[Poison/Legends|poisonous]] vapors emitted from the ground in swamps.<ref name="Roman">{{WebCite|url=http://www.unrv.com/culture/minor-roman-god-list.php|text=UNRV Roman Empire|archivedate=20100328104158|nolive=1}}</ref> The Mephitisians and their homeworld possess names similar to this goddess and share traits of hers on account of the planet's noxious atmosphere.<ref name="CC5" />
